title: Learning produces an orthogonalized state machine in the hippocampus
methods: two-photon random access mesoscope calcium imaging
clone-structured causal graph (CSCG) with Baum-Welch EM
data_sources: GCaMP6f two-photon calcium imaging (in-house, Janelia Research Campus)
Computational model simulation logs (CSCG, RNNs, LSTM, transformer, Hebbian-RNN)

data_scope.animal_subjects: 11 transgenic mice (GCaMP6f in CA1 pyramidal neurons); 3 additional for task-adaptation experiments
data_scope.imaging_modality: two-photon random access mesoscope, 5-mm FOV, dorsal CA1
data_scope.model_simulation_runs: up to 47 per architecture (CSCG n=20, polynomial softmax RNN n=47)

core_conclusion: As mice learn the 2ACDC virtual-reality memory task, hippocampal CA1 neurons progressively decorrelate their activity for visually identical but task-distinct states, forming an orthogonalized state machine (OSM). Among all computational models tested, only the clone-structured causal graph (CSCG), a hidden Markov model variant trained by expectation-maximization, reproduces both the final orthogonalized representations and the ordered step-by-step decorrelation trajectory, identifying hidden state inference as a fundamental algorithmic principle of hippocampal cognitive map formation.
why_it_matters: Provides the first longitudinal, large-scale trajectory of cognitive map formation at cellular resolution; constrains the algorithm (not just anatomy) of hippocampal learning; rules out LSTMs and transformers as adequate hippocampus models; demonstrates flexible and rapid OSM reuse for novel cues; bridges neuroscience and AI world-model design.
why_nature: Asserts a universal computational principle (hidden state inference) applicable to both biological and artificial intelligence; enabled by methodological frontier (large-scale longitudinal two-photon mesoscopy); cross-disciplinary impact; open-science infrastructure (Figshare, GitHub, interactive visualization).
added_value_to_my_research: CSCG clone-state architecture -> latent trip-purpose inference from ambiguous GPS/mobile-phone sequences in urban mobility modeling
Decorrelation-sequence benchmark -> evaluation criterion for whether geospatial foundation model embeddings orthogonalize functionally distinct but visually similar locations
Local Hebbian plasticity proof-of-concept -> privacy-compatible federated learning rule for spatial health-accessibility models with no data centralization
OSM 2D tuning-phenotype continuum -> data-driven splitter-unit score for GeoAI embeddings distinguishing ambiguous from robustly-encoded urban spatial units
OSM flexible reuse (~3x faster transfer) -> biological justification for frozen-backbone + city-specific adapter architecture in cross-city transfer of R2SFCA models

related.title: Reinforcement learning in artificial and biological systems
related.relation: reviews the biological-to-AI learning concept flow that motivates comparing hippocampal and RL learning rules

related.title: Resistive memory-based zero-shot liquid state machine for multimodal event data learning
related.relation: parallel state-machine approach in neuromorphic hardware, contrasting with the hippocampal OSM's biological implementation

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: spiking neural networks with biologically plausible dynamics, directly comparable to the paper's Hebbian-RNN

related.title: Network of evolvable neural units can learn synaptic learning rules and spiking dynamics
related.relation: meta-learning of synaptic plasticity rules, extending the paper's finding that architectural choices drive representation quality

related.title: Spatial planning of urban communities via deep reinforcement learning
related.relation: applies learned sequential decision-making to spatial planning, bridging the OSM's world-model insights to geospatial AI

related.title: Neighborhood street connectivity and hippocampus volume in older adults
related.relation: cross-disciplinary bridge showing that urban environments directly shape hippocampal structure, linking this paper's computational findings to public health

figures.caption: Mice exhibit systematic behavioural strategy changes (random -> both-rewards -> lick-stop -> expert) during 2ACDC task learning, quantified by coefficient of partial determination
Progressive decorrelation of CA1 population vectors across learning sessions and UMAP trajectories showing neural manifold evolving to a split-shank ring (OSM)
Single-cell tuning changes during learning: neurons shift from multi-region place responses to trial-type-specific splitter responses; 2D phenotype continuum revealed
Representational structure in mice vs seven computational models; only the CSCG replicates both the final OSM and the ordered decorrelation sequence
State machines can be flexibly used in novel settings: novel indicator cues learned ~3x faster; track stretching shows state-locked place fields reflecting latent state inference
